name: Dianne Reeves
abstract: Dianne Elizabeth Reeves (born October 23, 1956) is an American jazz singer. Reeves has won five Grammy awards from a total nine nominations.

about_quotes.speaker: Terri Lyne Carrington
about_quotes.evidence: we met and became really close friends and she still is one of my best friends to this day.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: when I met uh, Dianne, she told me that she was the niece of Charlie Burrell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: we had Dianne Reeves as a guest with us. And we recorded there live... So he and I are together on there along with Dianne Reeves.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: My wife just said to me this morning... 'Dianne Reeves, I talked to her at the Hollywood Bowl.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Glenn Zottola
about_quotes.evidence: he was 15 years with Dianne Reeves... So we did three singles
about_quotes.doc_title: Glenn Zottola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 7, 2020
